Value: Acute exacerbation of COPD with community-acquired pneumonia
Progressive dyspnea x3 days with fever and productive yellow sputum
66-year-old male with known COPD presenting with progressive dyspnea over 3 days with yellow sputum and fever 38.8C.
Father: COPD deceased age 72; Mother: DM HTN
Alert mild respiratory distress using accessory muscles
Regular rate and rhythm no murmur
Decreased breath sounds bilaterally diffuse expiratory wheezing coarse crackles at right base
No clubbing mild pitting edema bilateral ankles
Alert and oriented x3 no focal neurological deficits
Right lower lobe infiltrate consistent with pneumonia bilateral hyperinflation
Acute exacerbation of COPD: Methylprednisolone 40mg IV q8h x5d; Salbutamol nebulization q4h; O2 to SpO2>92%
Community-acquired pneumonia RLL: Levofloxacin 750mg IV qd (penicillin allergy); Monitor CRP; Repeat CXR 48h
Hypoxemic respiratory failure: Continuous SpO2 monitoring; ABG in 6h; Consider NIV if worsening
Uncontrolled T2DM: Hold metformin; Sliding scale insulin; Blood glucose monitoring q6h
